name: behavioural_assumptions.md
description: Comprehensive behavioural assumptions document including pass‑through, core deposits, prepayment, and approvals log.
content:
## Behavioral Assumptions Documentation

### 1. Pass‑Through (Non‑Maturity Deposits) Assumptions

| Assumption | Parameter | Justification |
|---|---|---|
| **Demand Deposit Beta (Pass‑Through Rate)** | 60% (short‑term), 45% (long‑term) | Retail deposits exhibit incomplete pass‑through due to franchise value and customer inertia. Short‑term beta is higher as rates move quickly; long‑term beta is lower owing to competitive stickiness. EBA/BCBS guidelines suggest 40–80% range. |
| **Deposit Rate Floor** | 0.00% | Nominal rates cannot go below zero in practice for retail depositors; zero floor is consistent with regulatory expectation and observed behaviour. |
| **Deposit Rate Cap** | 100% of policy rate | Pass‑through above policy rate is unlikely for non‑maturity retail deposits; competitive pressure caps at policy rate. |
| **Decay / Run‑off Rate (stable portion)** | 3% p.a. (annual decay) | Empirical studies show core deposit balances decay slowly (2–5% p.a.) even in rising‑rate environments. A 3% annual decay is a standard midpoint assumption. |
| **New Money / Balance Growth** | 2% p.a. | Matches long‑run nominal GDP growth / deposit expansion. Conservative; avoids overstating NII. |

### 2. Core Deposit Assumptions

| Assumption | Parameter | Justification |
|---|---|---|
| **Core Deposit Classification** | 70% of total non‑maturity deposits | Based on historical balance stability analysis (lowest balance over trailing 12 months / average balance). EBA allows 60–85% depending on granularity of historical data. |
| **Core Decay Half‑Life** | 5 years | Represents the median observed in industry behavioural studies for retail core deposits. Implies 13% annual decay rate (exponential). Aligns with EBA RTS on SOT. |
| **Non‑Core (Volatile) Portion Beta** | 90% | Highly rate‑sensitive; nearly full pass‑through. These depositors chase yield and reprice quickly. |
| **Core Deposit Rate Elasticity** | 0.3 (low) | Core depositors are relationship‑based and less sensitive to rate changes. A 1% rate change leads to only 0.3% change in balance. |
| **Replicating Portfolio Maturity** | 4 years average | Matches the average duration of core deposit liabilities under the replicating portfolio approach (EBA guidelines suggest 3–5 years for retail). |

### 3. Prepayment Assumptions

| Assumption | Parameter | Justification |
|---|---|---|
| **Base CPR (Constant Prepayment Rate)** | 6% p.a. | Represents a standard fixed‑rate mortgage pool with moderate seasoning. Based on industry prepayment models for prime residential mortgages (6–10% typical range). |
| **Incentive Effect (Rate spread sensitivity)** | +5% CPR per 100bp decline in rates | Historical prepayment data shows a ~5% increase in CPR for every 1% drop in mortgage rates below coupon. |
| **Seasoning Ramp** | 0–30 months SMM ramp | Prepayment speeds increase with loan age (0–30 months) as per PSA / SMM convention; fully seasoned at month 30. |
| **SMM Multiplier for High‑Risk Loans** | 1.5× | Buy‑to‑let / high LTV loans prepay faster; conservative overlay for risk differentiation. |
| **Floor CPR (no‑incentive)** | 2% p.a. | Even when out‑of‑the‑money, loans prepay for non‑rate reasons (move, sell, refinance for cash‑out). 2% is a standard minimum. |
| **Cap CPR** | 30% p.a. | Empirical observation that prepayment speeds saturate around 30% even with extreme rate drops due to friction costs and borrower inertia. |

### 4. Approvals Log

| Assumption Category | Owner | Approval Date | Approver | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Pass‑Through Assumptions | IRRBB Analytics | 2025‑02‑10 | Head of ALM | Approved |
| Core Deposit Assumptions | IRRBB Analytics | 2025‑02‑10 | Head of ALM | Approved |
| Prepayment Assumptions | IRRBB Analytics | 2025‑02‑10 | Head of ALM | Approved |
| Overall Methodology | IRRBB Analytics | 2025‑02‑12 | CRO | Pending |
